abstract: This paper examines the recent upheavals in the professional field of trust and safety (T&S), which encompasses content moderation and online safety work at technology platforms. Drawing on interviews with T&S practitioners, the authors document how political pressures, layoffs, and shifting platform priorities have destabilized the field. The study considers what these changes mean for the future of content moderation and the broader project of maintaining safer online spaces.
key_claims: The professional field of trust and safety is undergoing significant upheaval that threatens its continued viability in its current form.
External political and economic pressures, alongside internal platform decisions, are reshaping how online safety work is conducted.
These disruptions have meaningful consequences for the future of content moderation practices and online safety outcomes.
methods: Qualitative interview-based research with trust and safety professionals
Analysis of contemporary shifts in the T&S industry and platform practices
findings: T&S practitioners report destabilization in their field through layoffs, restructuring, and changing mandates
Political pressure and shifts in platform leadership priorities are key drivers of changes to online safety work
The professional infrastructure supporting content moderation is being eroded in ways that may affect platform safety
contributions: Provides an empirical account of the contemporary crisis in trust and safety as a professional field
Documents practitioner perspectives on industry-wide changes affecting content moderation
Frames trust and safety as a profession worthy of study in its own right, not just as a platform function
framing: The paper situates itself within content moderation studies, platform governance, and the sociology of professional work, treating trust and safety as an emergent profession. It engages with ongoing debates about the future of online safety amid political backlash against content moderation and corporate retrenchment from safety investments.
